refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation and task execution efficiency.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and outcome-focused; refining it into sub-FRs clarifies specific functional aspects that contribute to productivity, enabling targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations and compare against the customer-defined target; verify sub-FRs by assessing equipment operation efficiency and task execution metrics.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operational parameters to maximize throughput.
FR2: Efficiently schedule and execute worksite tasks to minimize idle time.
FR3: Provide real-time productivity feedback to operators and managers.

candidate_dps: DP1: Adaptive equipment control algorithms to optimize operational parameters.
DP2: Task scheduling and dispatch system integrated with worksite management.
DP3: Real-time telemetry and dashboard for productivity monitoring.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Adaptive equipment control algorithms to optimize operational parameters.

potential_sources: Interaction between control algorithms and task scheduling
Dependency on telemetry data for feedback
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically separable but requires interfaces with task scheduling and telemetry for feedback loops; coupling is manageable with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate sensor data for control algorithms.
Risk of suboptimal performance if task scheduling is not well integrated.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit coordination mechanisms with task scheduling DP in next decomposition level to reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded control systems responsible for equipment operation and control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends operational data and performance metrics to external monitoring modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task execution commands and schedules from higher-level systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as control algorithms are embedded in vehicle systems; low diffusion risk.
Broader scope but less direct containment of control logic; higher diffusion risk.
Minimal containment; control algorithms not primarily implemented here.
No containment; unrelated to productivity control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Embedding adaptive control algorithms within the Vehicle module ensures tight integration with physical equipment and minimizes interface complexity.
containment_risks: Potential difficulty in updating control algorithms if tightly coupled with vehicle hardware.
Risk of insufficient coordination with task scheduling if interfaces are not well defined.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts for Tasking and Telemetry to ensure modularity and ease of updates.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (adaptive control algorithms) logically satisfies the refined FR of optimizing equipment operation to meet productivity targets.

diffusion_risks: Moderate risk of diffusion if control logic is spread across multiple vehicle subsystems.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment scores, supporting effective packaging of DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring productivity metrics and validating control algorithm performance within the Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of control functionality to Vehicle module with defined interfaces to other modules.

recommended_revisions: Enhance interface definitions between Vehicle and Tasking modules to reduce coupling.
Plan for modular updates of control algorithms to mitigate containment risks.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are generally sound, refinement of interface definitions and explicit coordination with task scheduling DP is needed to reduce coupling and diffusion risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operational parameters to maximize throughput.
FR2: Efficiently schedule and execute worksite tasks to minimize idle time.
FR3: Provide real-time productivity feedback to operators and managers.

revise.why: To explicitly incorporate coordination mechanisms with task scheduling and telemetry feedback to reduce coupling and improve modularity.

assumptions: Accurate sensor data is available for control algorithms.
Task scheduling system will be developed in parallel and integrated.
open_risks: Potential integration challenges between control algorithms and task scheduling.
Difficulty in updating embedded control logic within Vehicle module.
evidence_needed: Performance data from prototype control algorithms during representative operations.
Interface specifications and integration test results with Tasking and Telemetry modules.
